title: 破镜谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\137-破镜谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Miss Marple discusses her observations with Mr. Cladock and Coddle, noting that her study of gossip magazines has provided her with insights into human nature and scandals. She suggests that the individual attempting to kill Marina Gregg likely possesses an abnormal psychology, drawing a comparison to Alfred, the son of Mrs. Pike, who is currently in Ferewyse Asylum.

The conversation shifts to the deceased Italian butler, Giuseppe. Coddle reveals that on the day of his death, Giuseppe arrived in London at 11:30 a.m. and deposited £500 in cash into his bank account at 1:33 p.m. without visiting any relatives. Miss Marple posits that this sum might be an installment of a larger payment, suggesting the killer could be a person of humble background working at the movie company, such as a servant or gardener, or perhaps a hired assassin. Cladock notes that Adwick Fenn, Lola Brutus, and Margaret Bens all traveled to London that day and were unaccounted for during the hours Giuseppe was in the city.

Miss Marple expresses suspicion that the motive involves the children Marina Gregg adopted, leading to a discussion on how childhood trauma and intense emotions can manifest in adulthood. After Miss Knight enters to serve tea and expresses concern about discussing murder, Detective Craddock assures her that Miss Marple is composed. Once Miss Knight leaves, Miss Marple asks Coddle to review the party guests and specifically inquires about the photographs taken by Margaret Bens on the stairs. Coddle produces one of the photographs for her to examine.
